description: On the Passenger details page, when the user changes the 'Number of passengers' dropdown (e.g. from 3 to 4) and fills all passenger fields, the form submits but only the initial count is sent. The seat selection and review pages show the old count (e.g. 3 passengers) instead of the new count (e.g. 4).
expectedOutcome: When the user changes the number of passengers and fills in all displayed passenger fields, the submitted data must match the current count; the seat selection and review pages should show that same number of passengers.
hint: The submit handler uses a ref that is set once and never updated when the passenger count changes; the payload is sliced to that stale count so the form state update (new passenger count) is ignored.
symptom: After increasing the number of passengers via the dropdown and filling the form, the next pages (seats, review) show the original passenger count, not the updated one.
solution: Keep the submitted passenger count in sync with the current passenger count: update the ref when passengerCount changes, and in onSubmit use the full data.passengers array (or slice to passengerCount) instead of slicing to the ref.
buggyCode:
const submittedCountRef = useRef(initialCount);  // never updated
  // in onSubmit: const toSubmit = raw.slice(0, submittedCountRef.current);
